title: Boat Rental in Vigo: Prices, Costs and Budget Guide 2026
html:
Renting a boat in Vigo on a budget is realistic, with most trips leaving from the Real Club Náutico de Vigo marina beside the city centre. Shared ría tours start near EUR 35 per seat, while a small motorboat day sits around EUR 250. BoatHire24 lists local options for every wallet.

Vigo sits at the head of its own ría, the broadest of the Rías Baixas in Galicia, and that sheltered water makes it a forgiving place to get afloat. The reward at the mouth of the bay is the Cíes Islands, a protected national park that crowns most itineraries in 2026.

Where to set off in Vigo

Most rentals leave from the working waterfront along the city. The right base shortens your crossing and stretches your budget.

  • Real Club Náutico de Vigo: the central marina, walking distance from the old town.
  • Marina Davila Sport: a large modern marina in Bouzas with deep-water berths.
  • Cangas do Morrazo: a ferry town across the ría, a quieter pick-up point.
  • Moaña: a calm bay on the northern shore, close to the Cíes crossing line.
  • Baiona: the historic harbour to the south, a short hop from the Estelas islets.
  • Cíes Islands: the protected anchorage and headline destination, roughly 8 nautical miles from the city quay.

Which boat to rent in Vigo?

For most budgets a small motorboat or a shared catamaran tour gives the best value, while crewed yachts suit groups splitting the cost. The right hull depends on your guest count and how far you push toward the islands.

  1. Shared ría tour seat best for solo travellers and couples wanting the Cíes view, joins up to 12 passengers, from EUR 35 per person.
  2. Small motorboat (self-drive) best for families on a budget, seats up to 6 guests, from EUR 250 a half-day.
  3. Sailing yacht best for a relaxed full day under canvas, comfortable for 8 guests, from EUR 450.
  4. Catamaran best for stable cruising and easy swimming, holds up to 10 guests, from EUR 700 a day.
  5. Crewed motor yacht best for a special occasion, hosts up to 12 guests, from EUR 1,400; for the larger end, the Maiora 26m charter page shows how a flybridge yacht is priced.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Vigo?

A day on the water in Vigo ranges from about EUR 35 for a shared seat to well over EUR 1,400 for a crewed yacht. The bands below help you budget, and you can browse skippered charters in Vigo to check real listings against them.

  • A shared group tour to the Cíes Islands costs from EUR 35 per person in EUR.
  • A small self-drive motorboat for a half-day starts near EUR 250 in EUR.
  • A catamaran hired for a full day runs from around EUR 700 in EUR.
  • A crewed yacht with skipper begins near EUR 1,400 per day in EUR, fuel usually extra.

When is the best season to rent in Vigo?

The high season runs from June to September, when Atlantic days are warmest and the Cíes ferry quota fills fastest. Galicia faces the open Atlantic, so weather shifts quickly and an early start usually buys the calmest water. The local nortada, a steady summer northerly, is the wind skippers watch most.

Book your Cíes Islands landing permit before you sail in July or August: the Xunta de Galicia caps daily visitor numbers, and skippers confirm whether your charter already holds one. In Spain a licence is required to drive most powered boats yourself, though many rentals come with a skipper, so have the operator confirm the exact requirement for your boat.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 35 per seat to EUR 1,400 plus per crewed day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 4 hours to a full day
  • Guest capacity: 2 to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR
  • Headline spot: the Cíes Islands, about 8 nautical miles out

Questions about boat rental in Vigo

Do I need a licence to rent a boat in Vigo?

For self-drive powered boats Spain generally requires a recognised licence, but you can book a skippered charter and skip that entirely. The operator confirms which boats need a certificate.

Can I visit the Cíes Islands by rented boat?

You can sail to the Cíes Islands, which lie roughly 8 nautical miles from the city quay, but landing needs a permit because the archipelago is a protected national park. Many charters arrange that for you.

How many people fit on a budget rental?

Small self-drive motorboats in Vigo typically carry up to 6 guests, while shared tour boats and crewed yachts take up to 12. Confirm the exact capacity before paying a deposit.

What is the cheapest way to get on the water?

A shared ría tour seat from around EUR 35 per person is the cheapest route, letting you reach the Cíes view without hiring a whole boat. Splitting a motorboat between friends is the next best value.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Vigo or list your own, and comparing 2026 prices takes minutes on the BoatHire24 marketplace. If you own a boat berthed on the ría, listing it puts your free dates in front of visiting renters all season.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Vigo and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
